comment.body:
Holy Alliance is basically a new alliance involving the us / Russia / Middle East aligning against China. The article explains that. This past weekends post also explained how the far right and far left of US politics have alignments internationally. Far left dems aligned with euro and China. Far right republicans aligned with Russia and Middle East. 

Peace between Russia and Ukraine is what the Easter timeframe was about. The tariff topic is not tied to Easter but the tariffs indirectly aid the peace process (my interpretation of Alma’s idea so Alma please don’t get mad at me). She’s been saying the tariffs force the EU to the negotiating table. I assume that means Trump would ask the EU to withdraw support of the Ukraine, which then forced Ukraine to capitulate and give into Russias “peace” concessions

comment.body:
Yes, it’s a long shot. Not sure Trump can pull this off. 

On the other hand, the prolonged war has been bad for Russia, and a ceasefire deal would actually give Putin lots of advantages: a symbolic victory, de-facto annexation of Kherson and Zaporizhzhia, much needed time to regroup, ease of sanctions…

comment.body: Yes, but it will open up a lot of new problems domestically. Fact is, Russia has a war economy running hot. Once the war is over, this will collapse. Also he needs a foreign enemy to point to while he and his oligarchs are sucking the country dry. So he needs war economically and politically. If the war in Ukraine really would end, then only on his maximalist demands (so he can present a victory). And even then yoz can expect another war to break out in the net two years
